title: Benchmark result (Contract 3)
description: Aggregates over one run set (one manifest x N repetitions). Normative. Structural rules this schema enforces: (1) percentiles come from POOLED raw events — pooled_percentiles.method admits only 'pooled-raw-events'; averaging per-run percentiles is not expressible; (2) shed_rate and stall_rate are REQUIRED siblings inside the goodput block — goodput is never reportable without them (a high goodput achieved by shedding or stalling must be visible in the same object); (3) the validity block is required. The benchmark comparability rule (compatibility-policy.md §7) MUST be printed in every rendered report of this data.

links.description: Provenance links. A result without its manifest(s) and raw events is not publishable.

run_manifests.description: run_ids or URIs/paths of the benchmark-run manifests aggregated here.

raw_events.description: URIs/paths of the raw-event JSONL files the aggregates were computed from.

requests_per_second.description: Completed (status ok) requests per second over the measured (post-warm-up) window.

total_requests.description: All requests in the measured window, all statuses.

method.description: NORMATIVE: percentiles are computed on the pooled raw per-request data across all repetitions of the run set. Averaging per-run percentiles is statistically invalid and MUST NOT be done; no other value of this field is schema-valid.

pooled_event_count.description: Number of raw events in the pool after warm-up discard.

tables.description: Per-signal percentile tables. Client-side series (client_*) per the measurement-point definitions in metrics/metrics.md; itl_seconds requires series-form raw events (summary-only events cannot be pooled — record the gap under validity.threats_to_validity).

goodput.description: Goodput@SLO: rate of requests meeting ALL targets of the referenced SLO. NORMATIVE adjacency rule (encoded structurally): goodput is never reported without shed_rate and stall_rate in the same block — a system can inflate goodput by shedding load or by streaming with long stalls, and both must be visible next to the number.

slo_ref.description: Explicit reference to the SLO (slo.schema.json instance) the goodput was evaluated against. Goodput without an SLO reference is meaningless and not expressible here.

ratio.description: Fraction of ALL offered requests (including shed ones) that met the SLO.

shed_rate.description: Fraction of offered requests shed (status=shed). REQUIRED sibling of goodput.

stall_rate.description: Fraction of streaming requests whose max_stall_seconds exceeded the SLO's stall threshold. REQUIRED sibling of goodput.

stall_threshold_seconds.description: Echo of the SLO stall threshold used, for self-contained reports.

knee_estimate.description: Saturation-knee estimate: the offered rate beyond which the tracked signal degrades disproportionately. Null is permitted ONLY when the run set did not sweep arrival rate, and that absence MUST be listed under validity.threats_to_validity.

signal.description: Which signal defined the knee (e.g. ttft_seconds p99, goodput ratio).

method.description: How the knee was estimated (e.g. two-segment fit over the rate sweep).

cost.description: Cost aggregates with an explicit cost-profile reference. Null is permitted ONLY when no cost profile applies (e.g. mock-backend runs), and that MUST be noted under validity.threats_to_validity.

validity.description: REQUIRED validity block: a result that does not disclose its warm-up handling, run count, threats, and anomalies is not a valid result. Empty arrays are honest claims of 'none known', not omissions.

warm_up_handling.description: How warm-up was applied in aggregation (must be consistent with the manifest warm_up policy).

threats_to_validity.description: Known limitations: e.g. no rate sweep (null knee), no cost profile (null cost), summary-only ITL events, client co-located with server.

unexplained_anomalies.description: Observations that could not be explained. Invalid runs are invalidated and never published; anomalies in published runs are disclosed here.

comparability_note.description: Optional statement of the single declared experimental variable when this result is part of a comparison.

percentileTable.description: Percentiles of one signal computed over the pooled raw events. Values in seconds.

n.description: Pooled sample count for this signal.
